  "decision": "fail",

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Brazing completed without nitrogen purge, resulting in black cupric oxide scale inside the refrigerant lines."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately.",
    "Cut out the contaminated sections of the copper tubing.",
    "Flush the system to remove any loose soot/scale.",
    "Re-braze the joints while maintaining a constant low-pressure nitrogen purge (1-2 PSI).",
    "Perform a pressure decay test and deep evacuation to 500 microns."
  ],
  "rationale": "Brazing copper refrigerant lines without a nitrogen purge allows oxygen to react with the heated copper, forming copper oxide scale. This scale will flake off when refrigerant and oil circulate, clogging filter-driers, TXV screens, and compressor oil ports, leading to premature system failure.",
  "references": [
    "EPA Section 608",
    "ACCA Standard 5 (HVAC Quality Installation Specification)",
    "OEM Installation & Service Manuals"
  ]
}
